step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{B}
Clinical Reasoning: Steroid 5-alpha-reductase deficiency leads to underdevelopment of male external genitalia due to insufficient conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), resulting in ambiguous genitalia, but it does not result in true hermaphroditism. Hermaphroditism refers to the presence of both ovarian and testicular tissue in the same individual, whereas 5-alpha-reductase deficiency affects androgen action specifically without altering gonadal sex. Instead, affected individuals typically develop as males with delayed masculinization and may undergo gender-specific treatments accordingly.
